title: Clock-Shift Algebra Has an r-Dimensional Irreducible Representation
summary: If A and B obey AB = omega BA with omega^r = 1 primitive, then repeated action of A cycles the r eigenvalues of B and generates an r-dimensional irreducible representation.
assumptions: omega = exp(2 pi i / r) is a primitive r-th root of unity.
B is diagonalizable on the physical Hilbert space.
regime: Torus Wilson-loop algebra in level-r abelian Chern-Simons theory.
scope: Representation-theoretic step behind the r-fold torus ground-state degeneracy.

math_expressions.context: Clock-shift algebra generated by the two basic Wilson loops.
Repeated action of A cycles through the r eigenvalues of B.

failure_modes: If omega is not primitive, the orbit generated by A is shorter than r and the representation decomposes.
retrieval_hints: Use when the user asks why the torus degeneracy is exactly r and not merely at least r.
